Patch 070: NaN guard for UnitClip in iccApplyProfiles (CWE-758)

Upstream iccApplyProfiles.cpp UnitClip() passes NaN through to
(icUInt8Number)/(icUInt16Number) casts — undefined behavior per C++ standard.
NaN fails both v<0.0 and v>1.0 comparisons (IEEE 754 NaN != NaN).

Fix: if (v!=v) return 0.0; — the standard NaN self-inequality idiom.

8 of first 100 fuzzer corpus files trigger the upstream UBSAN at:
- iccApplyProfiles.cpp:559-560 (UnitClip → unsigned char/short cast)

56 active patches total. Next: 071.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: replace manual packadd with lze dep_of/on_require specs

Instead of calling vim.cmd.packadd() inside after hooks, use lze's
built-in dependency handlers:
- dep_of = parent  → lze loads the dep before parent's packadd
- on_require = mod → lze loads the plugin when require(mod.*) is called
- dep_of on lazydev.nvim → loads before blink.cmp in addition to ft=lua

New lua/plugins/deps.lua centralises all dependency specs:
  nui.nvim        dep_of noice.nvim
  promise-async   dep_of nvim-ufo
  blink-copilot   dep_of blink.cmp
  SchemaStore.nvim dep_of nvim-lspconfig
  nvim-treesitter-endwise dep_of nvim-treesitter
  plenary.nvim    on_require plenary

Also fixes redundant self-packadd inside resolved-nvim's own after hook.

colon, which failed when values contained '=' (anonymous records, ref cells,
quoted strings). Changed to IndexOf('=') since FSI output format guarantees
the FIRST '=' after ':' is always the type/value separator.

- 4 bug fix tests including property for =-containing values

commit.message:
Fix NES002 false positive on static classes (#135)

* Fix NES002 false positive on static classes

The NES002 analyzer unconditionally flagged every class declaration,
including static classes. Static classes are just containers for static
methods and don't involve heap allocation or objects, so they should be
allowed.

This fixes the CI build failure for the multifile sample, where
Display and Palette are static helper classes.

* Update pippin/AIProvider/AIProvider.swift

On timeout, sendSynchronousRequest throws but the underlying URLSessionDataTask is left running. This can waste resources and keep sockets open. Keep a reference to the task and call task.cancel() when the semaphore wait times out (and consider cancelling on early error paths as well).

deleteMemo always uses defaultRecordingsDir() to locate the audio file, even when a custom dbPath is supplied. If dbPath points at a different container/location, the computed audioPath will be wrong. Derive the recordings directory from path (e.g., deletingLastPathComponent) to stay consistent with VoiceMemosDB.init.

commit.message:
v2: energy-gated VAD, streaming TTS, local Whisper, converse tool

Major improvements:
- Energy-gated VAD: auto-calibrates ambient noise floor (~0.5s),
  requires speech to be 2.5x louder than background to trigger.
  Dramatically reduces false positives from background chatter.
- Streaming TTS: pipes Azure audio to aplay as chunks arrive
  instead of buffering full response — starts speaking sooner.
- Local Whisper: offline STT via faster-whisper (CPU, base model).
  No network needed, ~2s transcription after recording stops.
- Converse tool: signals voice chat loop intent to Copilot,
  prompting listen -> speak -> listen cycle.
- Shared record_with_vad() utility for DRY recording logic.

New dep: faster-whisper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

CHARTS-180: Fix legend value rendering and performance in BaseLegend (#47459)

* Initial plan

* CHARTS-180: Fix duplicate items.find() and truthy check for legend value

Co-authored-by: adamwoodnz <1017872+adamwoodnz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(charts): Make BaseLegendItem.value optional

The runtime code and docs already treated value as optional, but the
type declared it as required. This caused a misleading type guarantee
for consumers. Aligns the type and API reference with actual behavior.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Guard against empty string values rendering empty spans

Empty string values (e.g. from leaderboard legend items) would render
a span containing only a non-breaking space, affecting layout. Now
BaseLegend also rejects empty strings, and the leaderboard hook omits
value entirely instead of setting it to ''.

Made-with: Cursor

* perf(charts): Use index-based lookup instead of find() in BaseLegend

Labels and items are index-aligned via the ordinal scale domain, so
items.find() per label was redundant O(n) work each render. Use
items[i] directly. Add regression test to ensure value-label pairing
stays correct if alignment assumptions change.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Expand changelog entry to cover all legend fixes

Include empty string guard, optional value type, leaderboard hook
update, and index-based lookup in the changelog.

Made-with: Cursor

---------

commit.message:
Charts: Refactor Legend API — consolidate styling props and decouple from visx (#47454)

* refactor(charts): Consolidate legend styling props

The Legend component inherited 10 individual styling props from
visx's LegendOrdinal (shapeWidth, shapeHeight, shapeMargin,
labelAlign, labelFlex, labelMargin, itemMargin, itemDirection,
legendLabelProps, shapeStyle), leaking visx internals into our
public API.

Replace them with three structured style objects — itemStyles,
labelStyles, and shapeStyles — that group related concerns and
provide a cleaner, more idiomatic React API. This decouples
consumers from visx's prop surface so future dependency changes
don't break the public contract.

Updates Legend, LeaderboardChart, stories, tests, and API docs.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Move maxWidth and textOverflow into labelStyles

These props control label text rendering and belong with other label
styling concerns in labelStyles rather than as top-level Legend props.

Chart components (BarChart, LineChart, PieChart, PieSemiCircleChart)
now forward legendMaxWidth/legendTextOverflow through labelStyles.
The chart-level API (BaseChartProps) remains unchanged.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore(charts): Add changelog for legend styling consolidation

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Address PR review feedback

Omit unused `children` prop from LegendOrdinalProps since
BaseLegend never forwards it to LegendOrdinal. Reuse the shared
LegendShapeStyles type in LeaderboardChartProps instead of
defining it inline.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Correct Legend style type definitions

Widen LegendItemStyles.flexDirection to include 'row-reverse' and
'column-reverse' since BaseLegend uses 'row-reverse' internally
for vertical + end alignment. Without this, consumers who try to
pass those values get a TypeScript error.

Use CSSProperties['margin'] for margin types in LegendItemStyles
and LegendShapeStyles instead of manual `string | number`, for
consistency with LegendLabelStyles which already uses Pick from
CSSProperties.

Made-with: Cursor

* perf(charts): Extract duplicate items.find() in legend render

The legend value lookup was calling items.find() twice per label —
once to check existence, once to render. Extract to a variable at
the top of the map callback to avoid the redundant traversal.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Use valid justify-content default for legend labels

'left' is not a valid CSS justify-content value for flex containers.
Replace with 'flex-start' which has the same visual effect and is
the correct flex property value.

Made-with: Cursor

* Update projects/js-packages/charts/changelog/charts-177-legend-consolidate-styling-props-into-itemstyles-and

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(charts): Narrow BaseLegendProps to used visx props only

BaseLegendProps extended the full LegendOrdinalProps surface via
Omit, leaking visx props that BaseLegend never forwards. This is
inconsistent with every other chart component which defines its
own props and selectively imports visx types. A visx update could
silently widen or break our public API.

Switch from Omit to Pick, exposing only the 6 visx props that
BaseLegend actually uses: className, shape, fill, size,
labelFormat, and labelTransform.

Also update API docs to include row-reverse and column-reverse
in LegendItemStyles.flexDirection, matching the exported type.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Use index-based value lookup in legend render

items.find() by label text can fail when labelTransform changes
label.text, and the truthy check (itemValue &&) hides legitimate
values like 0. Use items[i]?.value for consistency with glyph
rendering, and null-check instead of truthy-check.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Fix stale defaults in legend API and docs

The API docs still referenced justifyContent: 'left' after the
code default was corrected to 'flex-start'. The feature docs
incorrectly stated both maxWidth and textOverflow are required,
but textOverflow defaults to 'wrap' when omitted.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Inline legend value lookup

Remove the extracted itemValue variable added in c44af61 and
inline the items.find() call at the render site. The variable
extraction was a premature optimization — the find is only
called once per label in the map callback.

Made-with: Cursor

* Revert "refactor(charts): Narrow BaseLegendProps to used visx props only"

This reverts commit 754a04759490c992396265086b1309154fdf4e48.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Rename Legend props to drop redundant prefix

The legend prefix on legendItemClassName, legendItemProps, and
legendLabelProps is redundant within the Legend component itself —
consumers already know they're configuring a Legend. Renaming to
itemClassName, itemProps, and labelProps creates a consistent
naming pattern grouped by concern (items/labels/shapes) and
decouples labelProps from the visx legendLabelProps naming.

Chart-level props (e.g. legendItemClassName on BaseChartProps)
retain the prefix since disambiguation is needed there.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Replace itemProps/labelProps with labelClassName

The generic pass-through props (itemProps, labelProps) formalized
an accidental pattern from the old visx rest spread — legend-level
attributes silently applied to every child element. No consumers
used this behavior, and the only real use case for legendLabelProps
was adding a class name.

Replace both with labelClassName, which mirrors itemClassName and
keeps the API minimal. Generic pass-through can be re-added if a
concrete use case surfaces.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Narrow BaseLegendProps to used visx props

BaseLegendProps extended LegendOrdinalProps via Omit, leaking visx
props (style, data-*, event handlers) that BaseLegend never
forwards. Consumers could pass these props without TypeScript
errors but they'd be silently ignored at runtime.

Switch from Omit to Pick, exposing only the 6 visx props that
BaseLegend actually uses: className, shape, fill, size,
labelFormat, and labelTransform. Also update changelog to reflect
the full scope of API changes in this PR.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Tighten legend text wrapping assertions

The existing tests only checked that items rendered (toHaveLength)
without verifying that labelStyles were actually applied. Now each
test asserts the expected inline styles (maxWidth, minWidth) on the
label text spans, catching regressions in style forwarding.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Align margin type in docs with source

The API docs showed margin as `string | number` but the actual
exported types use `CSSProperties['margin']`. They resolve to
the same values at runtime, but the docs should mirror the source
to avoid confusion when cross-referencing.

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Assert shapeStyles margin instead of width/height

visx's LegendShape uses the `size` callback to determine width/height,
overriding explicit values. Assert on `margin` instead, which visx
always applies directly to the .visx-legend-shape element.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
